questions.text: When we compare living things, what are we doing?
options: Looking at how they are similar and different
questions.correctAnswer: Looking at how they are similar and different

questions.text: What is one way all living things are similar?
options: They all need energy to survive
questions.correctAnswer: They all need energy to survive

questions.text: How are plants different from animals?
options: Plants can make their own food; animals cannot
questions.correctAnswer: Plants can make their own food; animals cannot

questions.text: What is an adaptation?
options: A special feature that helps an organism survive
questions.correctAnswer: A special feature that helps an organism survive

questions.text: Why do fish have gills?
What helps a bird fly?
What do all living things have in common? (Select all that apply)
options: They are all the same color

questions.text: What are some adaptations animals have? (Select all that apply)
options: Long neck to reach high leaves
correctAnswer: Long neck to reach high leaves

questions.text: How can you compare two animals? (Select all that apply)
What are adaptations in plants? (Select all that apply)
options: Waxy leaves to keep water in
correctAnswer: Waxy leaves to keep water in

questions.text: All living things are exactly the same.
Both plants and animals need water to survive.
Adaptations help living things survive in their environment.
A cactus has adaptations that help it survive in the desert.
Fish and birds breathe in exactly the same way.
Comparing living things helps scientists understand and classify them.
A special feature that helps an organism survive is called an _____.
_____ is when an animal's color or pattern helps it blend in with its surroundings.
Fish breathe underwater using _____.
A giraffe's long _____ helps it reach leaves high in trees.
Desert plants like cacti have _____ leaves to reduce water loss.
When we look at similarities and differences, we are _____ things.
Match each adaptation with the animal it helps:
Match each term with its definition:

definitions: Special feature that helps organisms survive
The place where an organism lives

correctAnswer.Adaptation: Special feature that helps organisms survive
correctAnswer.Environment: The place where an organism lives

questions.text: Why do polar bears have thick white fur?
options: To keep warm and blend in with snow
questions.correctAnswer: To keep warm and blend in with snow

questions.text: How are a cat and a dog similar?
options: Both are mammals with fur and four legs
They are not similar at all
questions.correctAnswer: Both are mammals with fur and four legs

questions.text: What is one difference between a tree and grass?
options: Trees have woody stems; grass has soft stems
questions.correctAnswer: Trees have woody stems; grass has soft stems

questions.text: Why do cacti have thorns?
options: To protect from animals and reduce water loss
questions.correctAnswer: To protect from animals and reduce water loss

questions.text: How are a butterfly and a bird similar? (Select all that apply)
options: Both can move from place to place
Both are exactly the same size
correctAnswer: Both can move from place to place

questions.text: A chameleon changing color is an example of camouflage.
All animals have exactly the same adaptations.
An eagle's sharp _____ and claws help it catch prey.
Plants that live in water have adaptations to help them _____ in wet environments.
What can we learn by comparing different living things?
options: How they are related and how they survive in their environments
questions.correctAnswer: How they are related and how they survive in their environments

questions.text: Which is true about living things?
options: They are all completely different with nothing in common
They share some characteristics but also have unique features
They are all exactly the same
questions.correctAnswer: They share some characteristics but also have unique features
